Failing test: "highly enchanted sword (enchantment 5) adds 30% surcharge" — expects 145
Current error: Expected 145, received 115 (enchantment ignored)
Passing tests so far: 10

Spec: items with enchantment ≥ 5 add a 30% surcharge on the item base premium.
Computation: 100 base + 30 high ench (30% of 100) + 10 first ins + 5 fee = 145.

Add high enchantment surcharge. Threshold is ≥5. Stack additively with other surcharges (cursed, etc).

Working dir: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-57-53_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking

content.text:
All 11 tests pass (10 previous + 1 new). 

Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Added `HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_SURCHARGE_RATE = 0.3` and `HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_THRESHOLD = 5` constants, and extended `computeItemSurcharges` to add a 30% surcharge on the item base premium when `enchantment >= 5`. Refactored the loop to compute the item base once and accumulate surcharges additively (so cursed and high-enchantment stack additively).

**Result**: All 11 tests pass (21 todo remain).

**Approach**: Minimal additive logic — both surcharges are computed against the same item base premium and summed. The previously-passing cursed test still passes (the cursed sword test uses `enchantment: 3`, below the threshold, so only the cursed surcharge applies).

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-57-53_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
